page: Hetzner Cloud ccx43 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by dedicated AMD EPYC Milan processors running at 2.0 GHz. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, supported by 62.0 GB of RAM, which equates to approximately 3.91 GB per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. Local storage consists of a 360 GB SSD, and the instance operates under a QEMU hypervisor. No GPUs are included in this configuration, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations position the ccx43 as a balanced performer. Single-core performance is a notable strength, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests scoring 3056.68 and single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching 13.85 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Multi-core and general computational benchmarks, such as the Geekbench multi-core score of 11289.0 and Passmark CPU score of 14695.3, fall within the average tier. Memory bandwidth is stable, recording 52.44 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 37.18 GB/sec for large block write operations.

For specialized workloads, the server handles small-scale language model inference efficiently, achieving 269.05 tokens/sec for text generation on a 135M parameter model. However, performance drops significantly on larger models, with 70B parameter prompt processing scoring a poor 4.17 tokens/sec. The dedicated CPU allocation makes this instance qualitatively cost-effective for sustained workloads requiring predictable CPU performance without virtualization steal, such as software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx43 is a dedicated CPU virtual server built on the x86_64 architecture, featuring 16 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors running at 2.0 GHz. Equipped with 62.0 GB of RAM and 360 GB of local SSD storage, this instance operates under a QEMU hypervisor without GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations align with mid-tier averages. The dedicated resource allocation provides predictable performance, making it qualitatively cost-effective for workloads that demand sustained CPU capacity. It is well-suited for software compilation, static web serving, database hosting, and light language model inference.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx43 is an x86_64 server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 62.0 GB of RAM, and 360 GB of SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx43 features 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 62.0 GB of RAM, and 360 GB SSD storage, optimized for predictable general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance delivering balanced memory, local SSD storage, and predictable single-core performance for sustained general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs and 62.0 GB of RAM
360 GB of local SSD storage with QEMU virtualization
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Predictable dedicated resource allocation for sustained workloads
Suitable for software compilation, databases, and static web serving
